rxn_class_summary: Triarylamine-mediated electrochemical [4+2] annulation of alkenes with diols to afford 1,4-dioxanes and 1,4-dioxepanes
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of triarylamine catalyst 3 to its radical cation I, which acts as a homogeneous single-electron oxidant (mediator) to oxidize the alkene; protons are reduced at the Pt cathode to H2

key_intermediates.name: triarylamine radical cation I (oxidized mediator, Ar3N•+)
β-hydroxyethoxy carbon-centered radical III (after diol addition and deprotonation)
carbocation IV (after second SET oxidation of III)

electron_flow: The anode oxidizes triarylamine 3 to radical cation I, which performs SET on the alkene to give radical cation II; ethylene glycol adds to II and loses a proton to form C-radical III, which is further oxidized by another equivalent of I to carbocation IV; intramolecular cyclization of the pendant hydroxyl onto IV yields the 1,4-dioxane 4, while protons are reduced to H2 at the cathode.

probe_reagents.name: cyclic voltammetry of 3, 1, and mixture
probe_reagents.observation: Catalytic current observed when 1 is added to 3 (Ep/2(3)=1.48 V, Ep/2(1)=1.68 V vs SCE), supporting mediated oxidation of alkene by 3•+

probe_reagents.name: less-oxidizing triarylamine mediators 3a, 3b and imidazole 3c
probe_reagents.observation: Mediators with lower Ep/2 than 3 gave drastically lower yields (5–44%), consistent with thermodynamic requirement for SET to alkene

probe_reagents.name: electrolysis of product 4 with catalyst 3
probe_reagents.observation: 4 was recovered in 96% yield; no decomposition to 5/6, showing 3 suppresses overoxidation of 4

probe_reagents.name: electrolysis of product 4 without catalyst 3
probe_reagents.observation: 32% of 4 decomposed, giving 1,3-dioxolane 5 (16%) and benzophenone 6 (7%), confirming direct anodic overoxidation pathway in absence of mediator

probe_reagents.name: iPrCO2H additive omission / acid screen (AcOH, EtCO2H, CF3CO2H, TsOH)
probe_reagents.observation: Removing iPrCO2H or substituting other acids decreased yield, suggesting acid promotes H2 evolution and protects I, solvent and alkene from cathodic reduction

notes: Authors invoke two sequential SET events from the same mediator I (radical-polar crossover via radical cation II then cation IV). No radical traps (TEMPO/BHT), no KIE, and no DFT were reported; mechanism is supported primarily by CV (catalytic current) and by control electrolysis of isolated 4 demonstrating that 3 protects the product from anodic decomposition to dioxolane 5 and benzophenone 6.
